title: Serial Monitor Debugging
subtitle: Read Sensor Data, Detect Noise & Filter Values
overview: Students learn how to use the Arduino Serial Monitor to view sensor readings in real time, identify noisy data, and use if-conditions to filter unwanted values. This session develops debugging and troubleshooting skills essential for IoT and robotics projects.

gallery.caption: Arduino Serial Monitor displaying sensor values

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Serial Monitor Tutorial
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to display values using Serial Monitor.

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Programming Basics
youtubeVideos.desc: Introduction to Arduino programming and debugging.

activity.title: Sensor Value Monitoring and Filtering
steps: Upload a program that reads sensor values.
Open Serial Monitor from Arduino IDE.
Observe values changing in real time.
Add if-condition to reject invalid values.
Upload modified code and compare results.

sampleCode.title: Filtering Noisy Sensor Values

quiz.q: What is the purpose of Serial Monitor?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor displays data sent from Arduino in real time.

quiz.q: Which function prints data and moves to the next line?
quiz.explanation: Serial.println() prints data and adds a new line.
Noise refers to unwanted fluctuations in sensor values.

quiz.q: Which statement helps filter sensor values?
quiz.explanation: if-condition checks whether a value is within an acceptable range.

quiz.q: What baud rate is commonly used in Arduino examples?
quiz.explanation: 9600 is the most commonly used baud rate.
Serial.print() sends information to the Serial Monitor.

quiz.q: Why do we debug sensor data?
options: To identify problems and improve accuracy
quiz.explanation: Debugging helps identify errors and noisy readings.

quiz.q: Which sensor function reads analog values?
quiz.explanation: analogRead() reads values from analog sensors.

quiz.q: What is the main benefit of filtering noisy values?
quiz.explanation: Filtering improves reliability and accuracy of measurements.
